title: Official description evidence
principles: Official description text may provide mechanical evidence when it explicitly states a behavior, activation condition, target interaction, handling property, or quantitative mechanic.
Evidence must be evaluated per extracted claim because one description may contain mechanical statements, promotional language, and unsupported comparisons simultaneously.
Each claim must preserve its source fragment, subject, property, condition, affected mode or component, and stated scope.
A claim remains qualitative unless it supplies a clearly identified property, value, unit, and scope.
Structured evidence takes precedence only when both sources describe the same property, mode, component, and condition and make incompatible claims.
Description text may validly extend structured data when it identifies a mechanic that the available schema does not represent.
Description-derived claims must remain traceable and must not be silently converted into normalized statistics.

structured_confirmation: The claim explicitly confirms a mechanic, value, or condition already represented in structured data.
It provides corroborating evidence without replacing the structured field.
qualitative_extension: The claim explicitly identifies a relevant mechanic or condition not represented by the available structured fields.
Preserve the claim as qualitative evidence with all stated conditions and scope.
explicit_quantitative_extension: The claim supplies a clearly identified mechanical value with sufficient property, unit, mode, component, and condition context.
Retain it as sourced quantitative evidence until it is independently normalized or validated.
promotional_only: The claim contains flavor, praise, comparison, or subjective language without a mechanically testable statement.
Do not derive weapon-analysis conclusions from this claim.
conflicting: The claim and structured data make incompatible statements about the same property under the same mode, component, and condition.
Preserve both sources and mark the discrepancy as unresolved.
different_scope: The claim and structured data describe different modes, components, conditions, or levels of detail.
Do not classify the difference as a contradiction.
irrelevant_to_base_profile: The claim describes an external synergy, user-specific interaction, or conditional transformation outside the base weapon profile.
Preserve the claim as contextual evidence without applying it to unconditional weapon behavior.

conditional_weapon_behavior: Preserve the activation condition and affected state exactly as supplied.
Do not generalize conditional behavior into the unconditional base profile.
structured_schema_gap: Accept explicit qualitative mechanical evidence when no structured field exists for the stated behavior.
Do not invent implementation details or quantitative values to fill the schema gap.
numeric_text_present: Treat a number as quantitative mechanical evidence only when its property, unit, subject, and scope are identifiable.
Do not normalize isolated numbers or promotional comparisons.
multiple_claim_types_present: Do not assign one evidence-quality label to the entire description when its statements differ in reliability or purpose.

exceptions: Do not infer hidden values from adjectives such as powerful, rapid, accurate, precise, or devastating.
Do not infer mechanics from the weapon name, visual design, or thematic language.
Do not let description text silently overwrite explicit normalized component data.
Do not expand a named mechanic beyond the behavior, subject, conditions, and scope explicitly stated.
Do not treat absence of a mechanic from the description as evidence that the mechanic does not exist.
